children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Clean Energy Application Centers of the Department of Energy are redesignated as the CHP Technical Assistance Partnership Program (referred to in this section as the “Program”).

children.label: A
children.text: the 10 regional CHP Technical Assistance Partnerships in existence on ;December 27, 2020

children.label: B
children.text: such other regional CHP Technical Assistance Partnerships as the Secretary may establish with consideration given to establishing such partnerships in rural communities; and

children.label: C
children.text: any supporting technical activities under the Technical Partnership Program of the Advanced Manufacturing Office.

children.heading: References
children.label: 3 References
children.text: Any reference in any law, rule, regulation, or publication to a Combined Heat and Power Application Center or a Clean Energy Application Center shall be deemed to be a reference to the Program.

children.heading: CHP Technical Assistance Partnership Program
children.label: b CHP Technical Assistance Partnership Program
i
children.text: building, industrial, and electric and natural gas utility professionals;

children.label: ii
children.text: State and local policymakers; and

children.label: iii
children.text: other individuals and organizations with an interest in efficient energy use, local or opportunity fuel use, resiliency, or energy security, microgrids, and district energy; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide project specific support to building and industrial professionals through economic and engineering assessments and advisory activities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Program shall make funds available to institutions of higher education, research centers, and other appropriate institutions to ensure the continued operations and effectiveness of the regional CHP Technical Assistance Partnerships.

children.label: I
children.text: the CHP installation database;

children.label: II
children.text: CHP technology potential analyses;

children.label: III
children.text: State CHP resource pages; and

children.label: IV
children.text: CHP Technical Assistance Partnerships websites;

children.label: ii
children.text: to produce and conduct workshops, reports, seminars, internet programs, CHP resiliency resources, and other activities to provide education to end users, regulators, and stakeholders in a manner that leads to the deployment of CHP technologies;

children.label: iii
children.text: to provide or coordinate onsite assessments for sites and enterprises that may consider deployment of CHP technology, including the potential use of biomass CHP systems;

children.label: iv
children.text: to identify candidates for deployment of CHP technologies, hybrid renewable-CHP technologies, biomass CHP, microgrids, and clean energy;

children.label: v
children.text: to provide nonbiased engineering support to sites considering deployment of CHP technologies;

children.label: vi
children.text: to assist organizations and communities, including rural communities, developing clean energy technologies and policies in overcoming barriers to deployment; and

children.label: vii
children.text: to assist companies, communities (including rural communities), and organizations with field validation and performance evaluations of CHP and other clean energy technologies implemented.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: C Duration
children.text: The Program shall make funds available under subparagraph (A) for a period of 5 years.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: c Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $12,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2025.
